ab: kiyan hari su sawni aw?
ch: M:你今天有空嘛?
en: Are you available today?

ab: huwa la pi?
ch: W:怎麼了?
en: What is the matter?

ab: muwa ni musa kung matisan isu.
ch: M:我想去拜訪你。
en: I would like to visit you.

ab: kiyan yayuwaw mu sawni ini kung akiy sali, sa’utu la ki.
ch: W:我今天有事不在家，改天吧。
en: I am out today. How about another day?

ab: kiyan hariy su sasan aw? halay misu hamkaniy babaw kinriyax.
ch: M:那你明天有空嗎?我下午去找你。
en: Then, are you available tomorrow? I’ll visit you tomorrow afternoon.

ab: aw ki, naw misu sali.
ch: W:可以，我在家等你。
en: Sure, I will wait for you at home.

ab: ma’ut kung maha musa balay i galay misu su kay aw? galan mu su sasaku na babiru.
ch: M:請問我可以錄音嗎?我想做紀錄。
en: Would you mind if  I do the voice recording? I would like to keep a record.

ab: aw ru! ungat ya’ih niya.
ch: W:好啊!沒有問題。
en: Sure! No problem.

ab: musa balay magan kung su limu aw?
ch: M:可以錄影嗎?
en: Would you mind if I do the video recording?

ab: ya’ih ku sinrakiyasan mu. ini mu tarayuy uziy ku tawbung mu, lahiy ki.
ch: W:我不好看，倉庫也沒整理，不要啦。
en: No, I am plain and the warehouse hasn’t been tidied up yet.

ab: nanu u pasingiy la ga asa la.
ch: M:那我拍照就好。
en: Then I will just take photos.

ab: lahiy pasiyasing uziy.
ch: W:拍照也不要。
en: No photographing either.

ab: mahaway su amalu su su pana’ut.
ch: M:謝謝你讓我訪問。
en: Thank you for accepting the interview.

ab: ungat ya’ih niya, nanu ku sapa’ut su ga pa’ut.
ch: W:沒問題，你想問什麼。
en: You are welcome. What would you like to know?

ab: ma’ut kung su nanu ku yayauwa su saku la’i su?
ch: M:請問您年輕時是做什麼的?
en: What do you do when you are young?

ab: salali ga minkung kinsat, hani la ga malax kung la.
ch: W:我以前是警察，但我現在退休了。
en: I was a police officer but I am retired now.

ab: nanu u misu hani ga kiyan ki su pamahun aw?
ch: M:那你現在有工作嗎?
en: Then, are you working at the moment?

ab: niya kung i ma’umah yuwaw pakaba’an biru.
ch: W:我在學校上班。
en: I am working at school.

ab: niya kung ma’ut maha u niya maki alang ku lala’i su aw?
ch: M:請問您的孩子也住在部落嗎?
en: Do your kids live in the tribe too?

ab: ini, niya maki i uwis kwara lala’i mu.
ch: W:不，我的孩子在都市居住。
en: No, my kids are all living in the city.

ab: aw su i utux nanak hi maki sali la?
ch: M:那你自己一個人住嗎?
en: Then, do you live by yourself?

ab: kiyan utux huzin ru utux kabahani mu. utux mu sali laha uziy.
ch: W:我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，他們也是我的家人。
en: I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

ab: kiyan utux ngiyaw mu uziy, kiya ku yanux psabays ga ini ka’ilang.
ch: M:我也有養一隻貓，有動物陪伴不會無聊。
en: I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

ab: muwa na ma’ut kung tikay kusa na gaga pasani na maliyap.
ch: W:我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
en: I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

ab: ini kapayux ku ba’un mu, siki la kiya la ba’un mu ga nanu syukaw misu.
ch: M:我知道的不多，但我能回答的都會回答你。
en: I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.

ab: kiya la sawun pasani ku babing mamu su ya’aya na aliyap aw?
ch: W:你們打獵的武器有什麼禁忌嗎?
en: Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

ab: mutu ini babingiy na kanayrin ku ya’aya aliyap miyan hani ki.
ch: M:我們打獵的武器不可以讓女生碰到。
en: Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.

ab: nanu u mutu balay sarasun maliyap ku kanayrin aw?
ch: W:那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
en: Then, can women go hunting together?

ab: ya’ih, mutu ini usa maliyap ku kanayrin hiya.
ch: M:不行，女人也不可以打獵。
en: No, women cannot go hunting either.
